title: dioscorea nipponica subsp. nipponica
intro: Dioscorea nipponica subsp. nipponica, also known as Japanese yam, is a perennial plant species in the Dioscoreaceae family, native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The plant's health benefits include potential improvements in cardiovascular health, immune function, and anti-aging, as well as its use as a natural diuretic and anti-diabetic agent. Dioscorea nipponica subsp. nipponica is commonly prepared as a dried root or extract, and can be consumed as a supplement or tea in herbal preparations.
